topic: Schumann 7.83 Hz stable — verification of EXP-377-pickup-ISS-1374 Bessel-zero-gap substitution
change_summary: Decider self-applied EXP-377-pickup-ISS-1374 (2026-05-15T09:17:42Z) replacing the c7 "Bessel-zero monotonicity plus domain containment" claim in WIN-038.detail_evidence (iv) cylindrical-waveguide-Bessel-modes sub-bullet of the quarter-wave-escape preemptive paragraph. New parenthetical: (a) requires j ≈ 3.285 for 7.83 Hz TM mode, (b) shows 3.285 is not a J_n zero with nearest zeros j_{0,1}=2.4048 and j_{1,1}=3.8317, (c) flat-cylinder TM spectrum has 5.73–9.13 Hz gap straddling 7.83 Hz, (d) explicit PEC mixed-BC (Dirichlet radial, Neumann top/bottom for E_z) disclaimer that strict containment-monotonicity argument is non-trivial and is NOT invoked, (e) preserves dome-publication-burden closing. ISS-1374 closed fixed-pending-verification.
our_argument_summary: WIN-038 (Self-Contradicted) unchanged at the spine: the dome's own H(r) = 8,537 exp(-r/8,619) km plus the dome's own quarter-wave formula f = c/(4H) gives a pole-floor minimum of 8.78 Hz, monotonically rising outward — 7.83 Hz is below the dome's own geometric floor at every radius. EXP-377 surgically replaces a flawed monotonicity sub-claim with a BC-independent Bessel-zero-gap argument inside the (iv) preemptive sub-bullet, leaving the lead pole-floor argument, the averaging backup, the WIN-002/WIN-029 triple-count, the transverse-spherical-globe closure, and the post-hoc verdict-text intact.

relevant_files: raw-text/monitor.py (referenced, not re-audited — EXP-377 is prose-only)
dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: Unchanged from c7: monitor.py has a static expected and a static observed value for Schumann 7.83 Hz; no external API fetch for this WIN. Code tags inherit from prior cycle.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None identified — patch is prose-only on detail_evidence.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Pre-existing tag (c2–c7). EXP-377 patched detail_evidence prose only; monitor.py was not modified. monitor.py compares static 7.83 Hz expected to static observed value with no external API call for this WIN.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Pre-existing tag. The 7.83 Hz Schumann fundamental is explained by standard globe ionospheric physics (Schumann 1952; Sentman 1987; Nickolaenko & Hayakawa 2002). The dome relabels the same frequency as a quarter-wave cavity resonance without producing a different numerical value. Patch does not affect this tag.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Pre-existing tag. 7.83 Hz first theoretically predicted in 1952 and first measured 1954; continuously monitored since the 1960s. The dome adopted the known value and labeled it a confirmed prediction. Patch does not affect this tag.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: Pre-existing tag. The quarter-wave derivation H = c/(4f) runs in REVERSE from observed 7.83 Hz to produce 9,572 km (WIN-029); it does not derive 7.83 Hz from dome geometry. WIN-038's own argument is that no mode formula the dome has published derives 7.83 Hz from dome parameters. The Bessel-zero-gap substitution sharpens but does not change this tag.

item_1_replacement_present_and_complete.evidence: The (iv) sub-bullet of paragraph 4 of WIN-038.detail_evidence now reads: "The exclusion of 7.83 Hz follows directly from the Bessel-zero structure of the cylindrical TM spectrum: a flat-cylinder TM mode at 7.83 Hz requires the radial eigenvalue j_{n,m} = 7.83 × 2π × 20,015 / 299,792 ≈ 3.285, which is not a zero of any J_n for any (n,m). The nearest Bessel zeros — j_{0,1} ≈ 2.4048 (TM_{01}, 5.73 Hz) and j_{1,1} ≈ 3.8317 (TM_{11}, 9.13 Hz) — bracket the required 3.285 with no other Bessel zero in between, so the flat-cylinder TM spectrum has a 5.73–9.13 Hz gap that straddles 7.83 Hz on both sides." The disclaimer about PEC mixed BC follows immediately. The stale "Bessel-zero monotonicity" / "domain containment" phrases are no longer present anywhere in wins.json (grep count = 0 for both phrases).

item_2_j_3_285_not_a_bessel_zero.evidence: Numerical verification: 7.83 × 2π × 20,015 / 299,792 = 3.2846 (text rounds to 3.285, ✓). DLMF §10.21 Bessel J_n zeros checked for n = 0..4, m = 1..2: nearest are j_{0,1} = 2.4048 (|diff| = 0.880) and j_{1,1} = 3.8317 (|diff| = 0.547); next nearest j_{2,1} = 5.1356 is 1.85 away. No J_n zero within 0.5 of 3.285. Verification: TM_{01} cutoff = 2.4048·c/(2πa) = 5.733 Hz (text says 5.73, ✓). TM_{11} cutoff = 3.8317·c/(2πa) = 9.134 Hz (text says 9.13, ✓).
item_2_j_3_285_not_a_bessel_zero.remaining_concern: The claim is specifically about J_n zeros (TM modes). It does not directly address J'_n derivative zeros (TE modes). The flat-cylinder TE_{21} mode (j'_{2,1} = 3.0542) gives 7.28 Hz, which is 7% below 7.83 Hz. The argument paragraph already names TE_{11} = 4.39 Hz earlier but does not name TE_{21}. This is a minor residual — the dome's explicit framing of Schumann modes is TM-type (analogous to standard Earth-ionosphere TM_{0,n}), so the TM-only restriction matches the dome's own claim. Flagged below as a minor hole.

item_3_pec_mixed_bc_disclaimer_foreclose.evidence: The replacement explicitly states the PEC mixed boundary conditions (Dirichlet on the radial wall, Neumann on top/bottom for E_z) and declines to invoke containment-monotonicity. This honestly rules out the flawed-monotonicity vulnerability the c7 reviewer flagged (hole 2). The escape-foreclosure of "perturbation lands an eigenvalue exactly on 7.83 Hz" is, however, NOT done by the disclaimer itself — it is done by the burden-of-proof sentence "the dome has neither set up nor solved this coupled eigenvalue problem" plus the broader paragraph-4 closing "There is no mode formula the dome has published, nor any it could borrow from globe physics or cylindrical-waveguide theory, that reaches 7.83 Hz from dome geometry alone." The structure is honest (we do not claim what we cannot prove) but a sophisticated defender can still note that the disclaimer + the assertion "any close perturbation thereof, simply do not land at 7.83 Hz" together require a perturbation-smallness assumption that is not justified for the dome's factor-10 H(r) variation. See hole 1 below.

item_4_no_regression_in_surrounding_text.evidence: I diffed the c7 detail_evidence against the current text. The patch span ran from "so the globe-fingerprint is imported regardless of the detailed spectrum;" through "...simply do not land at 7.83 Hz." The replacement preserves the parenthetical's open paragraph ("A defender may reply that the honest calculation is a coupled radial-vertical eigenvalue problem...") and the closing paragraph-4 sentence ("There is no mode formula the dome has published..."). The (i)/(ii)/(iii) sub-bullets are untouched. The lead pole-floor paragraph 1, the averaging-backup paragraph 2, the triple-count paragraph 3, the stability=ionospheric-physics paragraph 5, the detail_verdict_text post-hoc paragraph, and the detail_extra OTS note are all unchanged. text_fingerprint comparison: detail_evidence_length grew from 9,302 (c7) to approximately 9,560 (net add of ~258 chars), consistent with EXP-377's declared net_word_add of +140 words. No DATA blob, no chart array, no pinned count touched (per EXP-377 test_required: false).

holes_found.description: The substituted parenthetical retains an implicit perturbation-smallness assumption that is not justified for the dome's H(r). The new text reads "the Bessel eigenvalue table above (TM_{01} ≈ 5.73 Hz, TM_{11} ≈ 9.13 Hz) is the leading-order reference: those values, and any close perturbation thereof, simply do not land at 7.83 Hz." Two issues: (1) the dome's H(r) varies by approximately a factor of 10 across the disc (8,537 km at the pole down to ~837 km at the 20,015 km rim), which is NOT a perturbative regime — standard perturbation theory off the flat-cylinder basis does not apply at this scale. (2) The patch elsewhere correctly disclaims containment-monotonicity (because PEC mixed BC does not give Dirichlet monotonicity), and then immediately makes an implicit perturbation-smallness claim that is no more rigorously defensible than the monotonicity claim it removed. A physics-sophisticated dome defender would say: "You explicitly disclaim monotonicity, then import a 'close perturbation' framework. Your H(r) varies by 10×. What's the actual quantitative bound on perturbation magnitude vs. H variation? Without monotonicity OR a perturbation-validity argument, why couldn't a 10×-variation H(r) move a TM-like mode from 5.73 to 7.83 (a 37% upward shift)?" The exclusion still works in practice because the burden-of-proof sentence ("the dome has neither set up nor solved this coupled eigenvalue problem") is what does the real escape-foreclosure — but the "close perturbation" phrasing weakens the rhetorical force of the parenthetical by importing a smallness claim that is not justified for the dome's geometry.
holes_found.recommendation: Future minor-edit pass: either (a) drop "and any close perturbation thereof" entirely and rely on the dome-burden close, OR (b) replace "any close perturbation thereof" with explicit framing: "the dome's H(r) varies by a factor of 10 across the disc, so the coupled-eigenvalue spectrum is not a small perturbation of the flat cylinder; without an explicit calculation on dome H(r), there is no published mechanism that lands a mode at 7.83 Hz." Option (b) is mildly preferable because it converts the implicit smallness claim into an explicit acknowledgment-and-rebuttal. Not a blocker for ISS-1374 verification — the core spectral-gap argument and the dome-burden close survive.

holes_found.description: The patched parenthetical restricts the Bessel-zero-gap argument explicitly to TM modes ("the cylindrical TM spectrum"). It does not separately address TE modes that lie between 5.73 Hz (TM_{01}) and 9.13 Hz (TM_{11}). The flat-cylinder TE_{21} mode (j'_{2,1} ≈ 3.0542) gives c·j'/(2πa) ≈ 7.28 Hz, only 7% below 7.83 Hz. The argument earlier in paragraph 4 (preserved by the patch) names TE_{11} = 4.39 Hz but does not name TE_{21}. A sophisticated dome defender could pivot: "You claim a TM-spectrum gap straddling 7.83 Hz, but TE_{21} at 7.28 Hz is within potential H(r)-perturbation range of 7.83 Hz." The argument substantially survives this for two reasons: (1) the dome's explicit framing of Schumann modes is TM-type (Earth-ionosphere Schumann is the TM_{0,n} family), so the TM-only restriction matches the dome's own characterization; (2) TE_{11} = 4.39 Hz is already below the 5.73 Hz lower cutoff of the TM gap, so even if TE modes were admitted, the LOWEST mode (the dome's claimed fundamental) would still not be at 7.83 Hz. But the patch leaves a defender-pivot opening that a future EXP could close with a one-sentence parenthetical (e.g., "TE modes are not the dome's claimed Schumann family in any case, since Earth-ionosphere Schumann is TM_{0,n}").
holes_found.recommendation: Optional future EXP: add a one-sentence TE-mode parenthetical inside the (iv) sub-bullet noting that (i) the dome's claimed Schumann mode is a TM-type fundamental analogous to standard TM_{0,n}, and (ii) even if TE modes were admitted, the lowest TE mode (TE_{11} at 4.39 Hz, already named earlier) is below the TM gap, so the dome's claimed fundamental could not be TE_{21}. Not a blocker.

holes_found.description: Wording softening: the c7 parenthetical closed with the pointed sentence "The dome has not computed or published any coupled-eigenvalue result that lands at 7.83 Hz." The patch replaces this with the more general "the dome has neither set up nor solved this coupled eigenvalue problem." The new wording is broader (claims the dome hasn't done the work at all, not just that the answer fails to land at 7.83 Hz). This is a soft loss of specificity — the c7 wording was more directly responsive to the defender pivot "maybe the coupled answer is 7.83 Hz." The broader paragraph-4 closing sentence ("There is no mode formula the dome has published... that reaches 7.83 Hz from dome geometry alone") is preserved unchanged and continues to do the strongest exclusionary work, so the net force of the (iv) sub-bullet is essentially unchanged.
holes_found.recommendation: No action required — the paragraph-4 closing sentence carries the load. If the parenthetical is ever re-touched, restoring the specific "lands at 7.83 Hz" phrasing inside the parenthetical would marginally tighten the defender response.

holes_found.description: Carryover from c7 hole 4 (globe-fingerprint repetition). The patched text retains "so the globe-fingerprint is imported regardless of the detailed spectrum" alongside argument (ii)'s "the globe fingerprint is imported a second time" and argument (ii)'s disc-diameter identity. All three invocations rely on the single AE-projection identity disc_radius = π·R_Earth. EXP-377 did not address this carryover. Not blocking — same severity-minor flag as c7.
holes_found.recommendation: Defer to future prose-polish EXP per the c7 recommendation.

holes_found.description: Carryover from c7 hole 5 (Collin citation chapter). The reference to "Collin, Field Theory of Guided Waves, 2nd ed., Ch. 5" for the cylindrical-waveguide formula remains unverified — EXP-377 did not touch citations. Carryover from c6 hole 4 and c7 hole 5.
holes_found.recommendation: Defer to future citation-audit EXP. Resolution is a library check.

holes_found.description: Carryover from c7 hole 6 ("terminates at an open rim" wording in argument (iii)). Unchanged by EXP-377. The dome's cavity is closed at the rim with H ≈ 837 km, not open. The argument's force does not depend on rim openness but the phrasing introduces a factual imprecision.

Carryover from c7 hole 7 (paragraph density). Detail_evidence paragraph 4 grew by ~258 chars to roughly 9,560 chars total for detail_evidence. The fourth paragraph remains the densest single

in the WIN panel. EXP-377 did not address paragraph splitting.
holes_found.recommendation: Bundle with prose-polish EXP per c7 recommendation.

holes_found.description: Carryover from c7 hole 8 (H(rim) rounding inconsistency). "836 km" appears in argument (iii) parenthetical while my numerical computation gives 837 km. EXP-377 did not address this.
holes_found.recommendation: Future clean-up; pick 836 or 837 and use throughout. Within 0.2% rounding.

holes_found.description: Carryover from c6 hole 2 (heuristic-retreat defense not closed inside the WIN panel). The strongest dome-defender pivot against paragraph 4 as a whole is still: "the dome uses quarter-wave as a simplified order-of-magnitude heuristic, not as the exact resonance formula." Argument (i) of paragraph 4 closes this via WIN-029 cross-reference (the dome treats the formula as quantitative when claiming the 5.3% match), but the closure is across-WIN rather than inside the WIN-038 detail block. EXP-377 explicitly deferred this per c7 deeper_analysis. Still the highest-value future EXP target.
holes_found.recommendation: Future EXP: quote the dome's wins.html scorecard line "WIN-029 ... CONFIRMED (H = c/(4×7.83) = 9,572 km, 5.3% from model)" directly inside paragraph 4 argument (i) as the in-panel proof that the dome treats quarter-wave quantitatively. Higher-value than the carryover wording fixes.

stronger_arguments.description: Quantitative perturbation-magnitude bound. To convert the implicit "close perturbation" claim into an explicit argument, compute the leading-order shift in TM_{01} eigenfrequency from a flat cylinder to the dome's H(r) via Rayleigh-Ritz on the flat-cylinder basis. For a small H-variation δH/H, the leading shift in the radial eigenvalue is O(δH/H) times the axial-eigenvalue contribution; for the dome's δH/H ≈ 10, the perturbation is not small and the linear estimate breaks down. This makes the "close perturbation" wording untenable but does not save the dome — the non-small perturbation means the coupled spectrum could in principle drift anywhere, AND that's precisely why the dome bears the burden to compute and publish the actual coupled eigenvalues. The argument shifts from "perturbations don't land at 7.83 Hz" to "perturbations are large, the dome has not computed them, and therefore has no derivation of 7.83 Hz from dome geometry" — which is cleaner.
sources: Standard Rayleigh-Ritz perturbation theory: Courant-Hilbert Vol. I Ch. VI; Jackson Classical Electrodynamics §8.7 for cavity eigenvalue perturbations.

stronger_arguments.description: Scale-analysis upper bound on the lowest TM-like mode. For the dome cavity, the natural characteristic frequencies are f_radial ~ c/(2πa) = c/(2π·20,015 km) ≈ 2.39 Hz (set by the disc radius) and f_axial ~ c/(4H(0)) = 8.78 Hz (set by the pole height). The dome's lowest TM-like eigenvalue must lie above max(f_radial, f_axial) in any reasonable scaling — i.e., above approximately 8.78 Hz, the pole-floor. This is essentially the lead argument restated in scale-analysis form: the pole-floor at 8.78 Hz IS the natural axial scale, and 7.83 Hz is below it. This makes the (iv) sub-bullet redundant with the lead argument from a scale-analysis perspective, but it strengthens the closing if reframed as "the dome's coupled eigenvalue problem, whatever its detailed spectrum, has natural scales set by c/(2πa) ≈ 2.39 Hz and c/(4H(0)) ≈ 8.78 Hz; 7.83 Hz lies between these two scales and the dome has not provided an explicit reason its coupled spectrum should land there."
sources: Scale analysis of cavity resonators — elementary, Jackson Ch. 8.

kernel_of_truth.description: The EXP-377 patch correctly identifies that the c7 "Bessel-zero monotonicity plus domain containment" claim was not rigorous for the PEC mixed-BC eigenvalue problem (EXP-377-pickup-ISS-1374.json:curmudgeon_review). Faber-Krahn / Courant domain monotonicity holds rigorously for purely Dirichlet eigenvalue problems, but the closed cylindrical cavity TM problem has Dirichlet on the radial wall and Neumann on the top/bottom for E_z. The substituted Bessel-zero-gap argument (data/wins.json:WIN-038.detail_evidence) is BC-independent — it operates on the cylindrical-waveguide TM spectrum (radial Dirichlet only, set by J_n(j r/a) = 0) and does not rely on cavity-top boundary conditions at all. The numerical check (this review, item 2) confirms 3.285 is not a J_n zero. So the substitution genuinely strengthens the argument by removing a flawed claim and replacing it with a sharper one.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The substitution does not change WIN-038's verdict (Self-Contradicted) because the verdict rests on the lead pole-floor argument (data/wins.json:WIN-038.detail_evidence, paragraph 1: dome's own H(r) plus dome's own quarter-wave formula yields 8.78 Hz minimum at the pole, monotonically rising), not on the (iv) Bessel-mode sub-bullet. The (iv) sub-bullet is preemptive defense against a coupled-eigenvalue escape from the lead argument; tightening that defense from c7 to c8 does not affect the lead. The patch also does not affect the post-hoc verdict-text argument (74-year-old measurement, OTS-on-observation-side per data/wins.json:WIN-038.detail_verdict_text), which independently disqualifies the WIN as a prediction.

deeper_analysis:
EXP-377-pickup-ISS-1374 is a clean, surgical replacement of a flawed parenthetical with a rigorously stronger one. The patch passes all four verification checklist items declared in the queue reason — replacement present and logically complete (item 1, PASS), j = 3.285 not a Bessel zero (item 2, PASS), PEC mixed-BC disclaimer present and honestly declines to invoke containment-monotonicity (item 3, PARTIAL PASS), no regression in surrounding text (item 4, PASS). The verdict (Self-Contradicted) is unaffected and untouched: the patch operates inside the preemptive (iv) sub-bullet of paragraph 4 of detail_evidence, while the verdict rests on the lead pole-floor argument in paragraph 1 plus the triple-count and post-hoc closure.

The one moderate residual: the new parenthetical retains an implicit "close perturbation" smallness assumption inside the closing assertion "those values, and any close perturbation thereof, simply do not land at 7.83 Hz." The dome's H(r) varies by approximately a factor of 10 across the disc (8,537 km pole vs. 837 km rim), which is not a perturbative regime. The patch elsewhere correctly disclaims containment-monotonicity, then immediately leans on a perturbation-smallness intuition that is not better-founded than the monotonicity claim removed. The escape-foreclosure still works because the burden-of-proof closing sentence ("the dome has neither set up nor solved this coupled eigenvalue problem") plus the broader paragraph-4 close ("There is no mode formula the dome has published... that reaches 7.83 Hz from dome geometry alone") do the real work, but the wording "close perturbation" could be tightened. This is the only newly-introduced rhetorical weakness I identified, and it is below verdict-flipping severity. I recommend minor_edit in a future polish EXP rather than blocking ISS-1374 verification.

The carryover holes from c6/c7 (heuristic-retreat closure, globe-fingerprint phrasing repetition, Collin citation chapter, "open rim" wording, paragraph density, 836/837 km rounding inconsistency) are all unchanged by EXP-377, as expected — that EXP was scoped to ISS-1374 only. They remain on the future-prose-polish docket.

Alternate-side note (per user preference to think about the other side): the strongest dome-defender reply to the new parenthetical is not the "perturbation lands exactly at 7.83 Hz" coincidence pivot (the patch handles that via dome-publication-burden), nor the "PEC vs. PMC BC choice" pivot (the patch handles that via dome's WIN-029 self-characterization as "Hard Conductive Ceiling"). It is the "close perturbation" wording weakness flagged in hole 1: a sophisticated defender will notice that we disclaim monotonicity and then immediately import a perturbation-smallness intuition that is not better-justified. The fix is to either drop the phrase or convert it to an explicit "H(r) varies by factor 10, so the coupled spectrum is not a small perturbation — the dome must compute it explicitly" framing. Worth a one-line polish in the next WIN-038 EXP.

On the TE-mode question (hole 2): I checked j'_{2,1} = 3.0542 → 7.28 Hz, only 7% below 7.83 Hz. This is the closest TE-mode cutoff to the target. The patch's TM-only restriction is honest (the dome's claimed Schumann mode is TM-type), and TE_{11} = 4.39 Hz already named earlier is below the TM gap, so the lowest mode of any kind isn't at 7.83 Hz either. A future prose-polish could close this defender-pivot with a one-sentence aside. Not blocking.

citations_verified: DLMF §10.21 Bessel zeros: verified j_{0,1} = 2.4048, j_{1,1} = 3.8317 to 4 decimal places, consistent with patch text. URL https://dlmf.nist.gov/10.21 resolves correctly.
Numerical: 7.83 × 2π × 20,015 / 299,792 = 3.2846 → rounds to 3.285 per patch text. ✓
citations_unchecked: Sentman 1987 (doi:10.1029/RS022i004p00595) — present in paragraph 5, unchanged by EXP-377, verified in prior cycles.
Nickolaenko & Hayakawa 2002 (ISBN 978-1-4020-0754-5) — present in paragraphs 4 and 5, unchanged by EXP-377.
Collin, Field Theory of Guided Waves, 2nd ed., Ch. 5 — chapter number for cylindrical waveguide formula still unverified (carryover from c6 hole 4 / c7 hole 5).

defense_rating.rating_scale: 0-3 scale: 0 = defense fully neutralized, 1 = minor residual, 2 = moderate residual, 3 = defense substantially survives

coupled_eigenvalue_escape.reasoning: Post-EXP-377, the coupled-eigenvalue escape is in the same neighborhood as it was post-EXP-211 but with the flawed monotonicity sub-claim removed. Residual defender moves: (a) the "close perturbation" smallness assumption is no better-justified than the monotonicity claim it replaced (this review hole 1) — moderate but answered by the dome-publication-burden close; (b) TE_{21} at 7.28 Hz is not separately addressed (this review hole 2) — minor, since the dome's claimed mode is TM-type; (c) the dome could in principle publish a coupled-eigenvalue calculation landing at 7.83 Hz, but has not — this is exactly the structural burden-of-proof argument the patch correctly relies on. Overall: defense substantially closed; one residual rhetorical weakness ("close perturbation") to clean up in future polish. Rating: 1.

summary_for_decider:
ISS-1374 verification PASSES. EXP-377-pickup-ISS-1374 cleanly substitutes the flawed Bessel-zero-monotonicity claim with a rigorous BC-independent Bessel-zero-gap argument plus an explicit PEC mixed-BC disclaimer. All four checklist items declared in the queue reason are verified (item 3 PARTIAL_PASS: the disclaimer itself does not foreclose the escape, but the dome-publication-burden close still does and is preserved). Numerical checks pass: 3.285, 5.73 Hz, 9.13 Hz, 8.78 Hz all confirmed. Stale phrases "Bessel-zero monotonicity" and "domain containment" no longer appear in wins.json (grep count = 0). Verdict (Self-Contradicted) is untouched. ISS-1374 may be promoted from fixed-pending-verification to closed-fixed.

One moderate residual hole flagged: the substituted text contains the phrase "any close perturbation thereof" which imports an implicit perturbation-smallness assumption that is not justified for the dome's factor-10 H(r) variation. Recommended action minor_edit (drop the phrase or reframe as an explicit acknowledgment that H varies by 10× and therefore the dome must compute the actual coupled eigenvalues). NOT a blocker — the dome-publication-burden close ("the dome has neither set up nor solved this coupled eigenvalue problem" + the paragraph-4 broader close) does the real escape-foreclosure work.

Minor TE-mode coverage gap also flagged (TE_{21} at 7.28 Hz is 7% below 7.83 Hz and not separately addressed), but the patch's explicit TM-only restriction matches the dome's own framing of Schumann as TM-type.

Carryover holes from c6/c7 (heuristic-retreat closure inside WIN-038 panel, globe-fingerprint phrasing repetition, Collin citation chapter, "open rim" wording, paragraph density, 836/837 km rounding) are all out-of-scope for EXP-377 and remain on future-prose-polish docket. The highest-value next EXP target on WIN-038 is still the heuristic-retreat closure (quote the dome's own WIN-029 "CONFIRMED (H = c/(4×7.83) = 9,572 km, 5.3% from model)" scorecard line inside paragraph 4 argument (i)).
